title: BlockableAndUnblockable
tex_block_html: Let $G=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG and $\pi$ a walk in $G$: \[ \pi =\lp v_0 \sus \cdots \sus v_n \rp. \] We call a non-collider $v_k$ on $\pi$ an unblockable non-collider on $\pi$ if it is not an end-node ($k \notin \{0,n\}$) and it only has outgoing edges on $\pi$ to nodes in the same strongly connected component of $G$. That is, it is one of the following patterns: \[\begin{array}{rccl} \text{ left chain: } & v_{k-1} \hut v_k \hus v_{k+1} & \text{ with } & v_{k-1} \in \Sc^G(v_{k})\\ \text{ right chain: } & v_{k-1} \suh v_k \tuh v_{k+1} & \text{ with } & v_{k+1} \in \Sc^G(v_{k})\\ \text{ fork: } & v_{k-1} \hut v_k \tuh v_{k+1} & \text{ with } & v_{k-1} \in \Sc^G(v_k) \land v_{k+1} \in \Sc^G(v_k) \end{array}\] Otherwise, $v_k$ is called a blockable non-collider on $\pi$. This means that $v_k$ is either an end-node ($k \in \lC 0,n\rC$) or it has at least one outgoing arrow $v_k \tuh v_{k\pm1}$ pointing to a node $v_{k\pm1}$ that lies in a different strongly connected component than $v_k$, i.e.\ $v_{k\pm1} \notin \Sc^G(v_k)$.

tex_statement.html:
Let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG (def def-cdmg); throughout we use the notation of def not-cdmg and the collider/non-collider machinery of def def:collider_noncollider. Let \[ \pi = (v_0, a_0, v_1, a_1, \dots, v_{n-1}, a_{n-1}, v_n) \] be a walk in $G$ (def def:walks, item i.) for some integer $n \ge 0$, with $v_0, \dots, v_n \in J \cup V$ and $a_0, \dots, a_{n-1} \in E \cup L$ satisfying the walk constraint at every $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n - 1\}$: \[ \bigl(a_k = (v_k, v_{k+1}) \;\land\; a_k \in E \cup L\bigr) \;\lor\; \bigl(a_k = (v_{k+1}, v_k) \;\land\; a_k \in E\bigr). \]

Strongly connected component. For $v \in J \cup V$ we write $\Sc^G(v) \subseteq J \cup V$ for the strongly connected component of $v$ in $G$, defined per the family-relationships definition (the LN block introducing $\Anc^G, \Desc^G, \Sc^G$ on graphs) as \[ \Sc^G(v) := \Anc^G(v) \cap \Desc^G(v); \] in particular $v \in \Sc^G(v)$. Membership $w \in \Sc^G(v)$ is the set-theoretic predicate ‚Äú$w$ is both an ancestor and a descendant of $v$ in $G$‚Äù.

Walk-incident indices and outgoing walk-edges at a position. Mirroring the index-set convention of def def:collider_noncollider, for a position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$ on $\pi$ let the set of walk-incident indices at $k$ be \[ I_\pi(k) := \bigl\{\, i \in \{k - 1, k\} \,\bigm|\, 0 \le i \le n - 1 \,\bigr\}. \] So $|I_\pi(k)| \le 2$: at an end-position $k \in \{0, n\}$ on a non-trivial walk ($n \ge 1$) we have $|I_\pi(k)| = 1$ (namely $\{0\}$ at $k = 0$ and $\{n - 1\}$ at $k = n$); on the trivial walk $n = 0$ we have $I_\pi(0) = \emptyset$; at an interior position $1 \le k \le n - 1$ we have $I_\pi(k) = \{k - 1, k\}$. For $i \in I_\pi(k)$, writing $a_i = (e_1, e_2)$, we say that $a_i$ is an outgoing walk-edge of $v_k$ at position $k$ on $\pi$ iff \[ a_i \in E \;\land\; e_1 = v_k, \] i.e.\ $a_i$ is a directed edge whose tail is $v_k$. Spelled out via the walk constraint at index $i$, the only ways this can occur are:

  • for $i = k - 1$ (requires $k \ge 1$): $a_{k-1} = (v_k, v_{k-1}) \in E$; its ‚Äúother endpoint‚Äù along $\pi$ is $v_{k-1}$.
  • for $i = k$ (requires $k \le n - 1$): $a_k = (v_k, v_{k+1}) \in E$; its ‚Äúother endpoint‚Äù along $\pi$ is $v_{k+1}$.

Bidirected edges ($a_i \in L$) and directed edges into $v_k$ (i.e.\ $a_i \in E$ with $e_2 = v_k$ and $e_1 \neq v_k$) are excluded from this predicate. Edges of $G$ that do not appear as $a_{k-1}$ or $a_k$ on $\pi$ are not considered: ‚Äúoutgoing walk-edge‚Äù is a walk-specific property, not an existence claim about edges in $G$.

Unblockable non-collider on $\pi$. A position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$ on $\pi$ ‚Äì or, by abuse, the node $v_k$ at position $k$ ‚Äì is called an unblockable non-collider on $\pi$ iff all three of the following hold:

  1. $k$ is a non-collider on $\pi$ in the sense of def def:collider_noncollider, item i.\ (equivalently, $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k) \le 1$);
  2. $k$ is interior, i.e.\ $k \notin \{0, n\}$ (equivalently, $1 \le k \le n - 1$, which forces $I_\pi(k) = \{k - 1, k\}$);
  3. for every $i \in I_\pi(k)$ such that $a_i$ is an outgoing walk-edge of $v_k$ at position $k$ on $\pi$, the other endpoint of $a_i$ along $\pi$ lies in $\Sc^G(v_k)$. Spelled out as two implications (with $1 \le k \le n - 1$): \[ \bigl(a_{k-1} = (v_k, v_{k-1}) \in E \;\implies\; v_{k-1} \in \Sc^G(v_k)\bigr) \;\land\; \bigl(a_k = (v_k, v_{k+1}) \in E \;\implies\; v_{k+1} \in \Sc^G(v_k)\bigr). \]

Blockable non-collider on $\pi$. A position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$ on $\pi$ ‚Äì or, by abuse, the node $v_k$ at position $k$ ‚Äì is called a blockable non-collider on $\pi$ iff:

  1. $k$ is a non-collider on $\pi$ in the sense of def def:collider_noncollider, item i.; AND
  2. $k$ is not an unblockable non-collider on $\pi$.

Unfolding the negation of clauses (ii)‚Äì(iii) of unblockable, the second condition is equivalent to: either $k \in \{0, n\}$ (end-position), or there exists $i \in I_\pi(k)$ such that $a_i$ is an outgoing walk-edge of $v_k$ at position $k$ on $\pi$ whose other endpoint along $\pi$ does not lie in $\Sc^G(v_k)$. Spelled out explicitly as a disjunction (the latter two disjuncts implicitly requiring $k \ge 1$ resp.\ $k \le n - 1$, and being vacuously false outside that range): \[ k \in \{0, n\} \;\;\lor\;\; \bigl(a_{k-1} = (v_k, v_{k-1}) \in E \;\land\; v_{k-1} \notin \Sc^G(v_k)\bigr) \;\;\lor\;\; \bigl(a_k = (v_k, v_{k+1}) \in E \;\land\; v_{k+1} \notin \Sc^G(v_k)\bigr). \] On the sub-class of non-collider positions of $\pi$, the unblockable and blockable classifications are by construction mutually exclusive and jointly exhaustive: every non-collider position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$ on $\pi$ is exactly one of an unblockable non-collider on $\pi$ or a blockable non-collider on $\pi$.

Reconciliation with the source-block pattern writings. The source block characterises the interior unblockable non-collider via a list of three pattern-cases (left chain, right chain, fork) glossed as ‚Äú$v_k$ only has outgoing edges on $\pi$ to nodes in the same strongly connected component of $G$‚Äù. Below we reconcile these visual writings with the walk-edge-based classification above, taking the walk-edge-based classification (the LN's ‚Äúi.e.‚Äù\ gloss ‚Äúoutgoing edges on $\pi$‚Äù) as canonical. The visual writings $v_{k-1} \tuh v_k$, $v_{k-1} \hut v_k$, $v_{k-1} \huh v_k$, $v_{k-1} \suh v_k$, $v_{k-1} \hus v_k$ are unfolded per def not-cdmg, items 2‚Äì6; each of the three patterns refers to an interior position $1 \le k \le n - 1$, so $I_\pi(k) = \{k - 1, k\}$.

  • left chain ($1 \le k \le n - 1$; LN writing $v_{k-1} \hut v_k \hus v_{k+1}$ with $v_{k-1} \in \Sc^G(v_k)$): only the walk-edge $a_{k-1}$ is outgoing from $v_k$ on $\pi$. Spelled out via the walk constraint, $a_{k-1} = (v_k, v_{k-1}) \in E$ (a directed edge with tail $v_k$), and either $a_k = (v_{k+1}, v_k) \in E$ or $a_k = (v_k, v_{k+1}) \in L$ (so $a_k$ is not an outgoing walk-edge of $v_k$). The SC requirement attaches to the unique outgoing walk-edge $a_{k-1}$, reading $v_{k-1} \in \Sc^G(v_k)$ ‚Äì matching the LN. The non-collider count from def def:collider_noncollider is $0 + 1 = 1$, consistent with the non-collider precondition.
  • right chain ($1 \le k \le n - 1$; LN writing $v_{k-1} \suh v_k \tuh v_{k+1}$ with $v_{k+1} \in \Sc^G(v_k)$): only the walk-edge $a_k$ is outgoing from $v_k$ on $\pi$. Spelled out via the walk constraint, either $a_{k-1} = (v_{k-1}, v_k) \in E$ or $a_{k-1} = (v_{k-1}, v_k) \in L$ (so $a_{k-1}$ is not an outgoing walk-edge of $v_k$), and $a_k = (v_k, v_{k+1}) \in E$. The SC requirement attaches to the unique outgoing walk-edge $a_k$, reading $v_{k+1} \in \Sc^G(v_k)$ ‚Äì matching the LN. The non-collider count is $1 + 0 = 1$.
  • fork ($1 \le k \le n - 1$; LN writing $v_{k-1} \hut v_k \tuh v_{k+1}$ with $v_{k-1} \in \Sc^G(v_k) \land v_{k+1} \in \Sc^G(v_k)$): both walk-edges $a_{k-1}$ and $a_k$ are outgoing from $v_k$ on $\pi$. Spelled out, $a_{k-1} = (v_k, v_{k-1}) \in E$ and $a_k = (v_k, v_{k+1}) \in E$. The SC requirement attaches to both outgoing walk-edges, reading $v_{k-1} \in \Sc^G(v_k) \land v_{k+1} \in \Sc^G(v_k)$ ‚Äì matching the LN. The non-collider count is $0 + 0 = 0$.
  • end-position ($k \in \{0, n\}$): excluded from the unblockable classification by clause (ii); the source-block elaboration assigns end-positions to the blockable category via the $k \in \{0, n\}$ disjunct, consistent with the disjunction above.
  • collider position ($\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k) = 2$, LN writing $v_{k-1} \suh v_k \hus v_{k+1}$): excluded from both unblockable and blockable by the non-collider precondition (clause (i) in either definition).

The visual shorthands $\hut, \tuh, \hus, \suh, \huh$ in def not-cdmg are statements about the existence of edges in $G$, not about the walk's specific incident edges $a_{k-1}, a_k$. The source block's verbal gloss ‚Äúoutgoing edges on $\pi$‚Äù fixes the intended walk-edge reading, which is what we have adopted above. In particular: a non-walk directed edge $(v_k, w) \in E$ of $G$ (one that does not appear as $a_{k-1}$ or $a_k$ on $\pi$) is irrelevant to whether the position $k$ on $\pi$ is unblockable or blockable; symmetrically, the negation of the unblockable condition in the source block (‚Äúit has at least one outgoing arrow $v_k \tuh v_{k \pm 1}$ ‚Ä¶‚Äù) is to be read as ‚Äúthe walk's $a_{k - 1}$ or $a_k$ is a directed edge $(v_k, v_{k - 1}) \in E$ resp.\ $(v_k, v_{k + 1}) \in E$ whose tail is $v_k$ and whose other endpoint along $\pi$ is not in $\Sc^G(v_k)$‚Äù, not an existence claim about a $\tuh$-arrow in $G$ unrelated to the walk.

Treatment of directed self-loops. As in def def:collider_noncollider, a directed self-loop $(v, v) \in E$ is admitted by def def-cdmg (the irreflexivity restriction applies to $L$, not to $E$). If a walk-incident edge $a_i$ is such a self-loop ‚Äì so $a_i = (v_i, v_{i+1}) = (v, v) \in E$ with $v_i = v_{i+1} = v$ ‚Äì and the unblockable/blockable classification is evaluated at a position $k \in \{i, i + 1\}$ for which $v_k = v$, then:

  • $a_i$ contributes exactly once to $I_\pi(k)$ (indexed by the unique $i$ with $a_i = (v, v)$); the walk-edge enumeration does not double-count self-loops.
  • $a_i$ is an outgoing walk-edge of $v_k$ at position $k$ on $\pi$ in the sense above (the predicate $a_i \in E \land e_1 = v_k$ fires with $e_1 = v = v_k$). The ‚Äúother endpoint‚Äù of $a_i$ along $\pi$ is again $v$, and $v \in \Sc^G(v)$ holds trivially, so this particular contribution to the unblockable-pattern check is automatically satisfied. (In particular, a self-loop alone never disqualifies an interior position from being unblockable; whether the position is unblockable depends on the other walk-incident edge ‚Äì if any ‚Äì in the standard way.)

The walk-edge-based unblockable/blockable classification above thus assigns a single, well-defined status to such a $k$ on $\pi$, regardless of self-loop traversals. The apparent overlap of the source block's visual ‚Äúleft chain‚Äù, ‚Äúright chain‚Äù, and ‚Äúfork‚Äù pattern conjunctions at directed self-loops ‚Äì where ‚Äú$v_{k - 1} \hut v_k$‚Äù and ‚Äú$v_k \tuh v_{k - 1}$‚Äù both unfold (per def not-cdmg, items 2‚Äì3) to the single condition $(v, v) \in E$ when $v_{k - 1} = v_k = v$, so several pattern conjunctions can simultaneously match ‚Äì is uniformly resolved by the walk-edge unfolding, inheriting the canonical resolution of def def:collider_noncollider.

lean_blocks.explanation: This is a `variable` declaration: Lean auto-binds `Node : Type*` and the instance `[DecidableEq Node]` as implicit arguments to every subsequent declaration in the surrounding `CDMG` namespace, sparing us from rewriting the same parameters on `IsUnblockableNonCollider` and `IsBlockableNonCollider`. The `Type*` notation makes `Node` universe-polymorphic, so callers can instantiate it at any universe level. The `DecidableEq Node` assumption is load-bearing for the classifier in particular: the predicate bodies destructure `Finset` membership for `G.E`, evaluate the Option-valued list lookups `p.vertices[k]?` and `p.edges[k]?` (which compute over `Node √ó Node`), and reach into `G.Sc vk` which is built from `Finset.image`/`Finset.filter` over the ancestor and descendant operators of `def_3_5` ‚Äî all of which need decidable equality on the element type. Stronger structure on `Node` (e.g.\ `Fintype`, `LinearOrder`) is not required here and is deferred to downstream consumers that actually use it. Same convention as the rest of `Chapter3_GraphTheory`'s files.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- Auto-binds `Node` + `DecidableEq Node` as implicits; decidability drives `Finset` membership and the `G.Sc` lookup below.
variable {Node : Type*} [DecidableEq Node]

lean_blocks.explanation: A second `variable` declaration scoped to the inner `Walk` namespace: Lean auto-binds `{G : CDMG Node}` as an implicit argument to every subsequent declaration, carrying the LN's once-at-the-top quantifier `let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG' through both predicate signatures without repeating it. Without this, every signature would carry an explicit `{G : CDMG Node}` binder before `(p : Walk G u v)`, since the walk type and the field projections `G.E` and `G.Sc` inside the bodies both reach into the ambient CDMG. The binder is implicit (`{G}`) rather than explicit because downstream consumers invoke the predicates via dot-notation on the walk (`p.IsUnblockableNonCollider k`), letting Lean infer `G` from the walk's type. This is the section-3.3 convention shared with `CollidersAndNon.lean`'s `IsCollider`/`IsNonCollider`.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- Auto-binds `{G : CDMG Node}` for every `Walk`-namespace decl; carries the LN's once-at-the-top CDMG quantifier.
variable {G : CDMG Node}

lean_blocks.code:
def HasBlockingLeftSlot : ‚àÄ {u v : Node}, Walk G u v ‚Üí ‚Ñï ‚Üí Prop
  | _, _, .nil _ _, _ => False
  | _, _, .cons _ _ _, 0 => False
  | u, _, .cons v (.backwardE _) _, 1 => u ‚àâ G.Sc v
  | _, _, .cons _ (.forwardE _) _, 1 => False
  | _, _, .cons _ (.bidir _) _, 1 => False
  | _, _, .cons _ _ p, k + 2 => p.HasBlockingLeftSlot (k + 1)
lean_blocks.explanation: A recursive helper that decides whether the slot $i = k - 1$ on `p` ‚Äî the WalkStep that ENDS at outer position $k$ ‚Äî is an outgoing E-walk-edge of $v_k$ whose other walk-endpoint lies outside $G.Sc\, v_k$. The helper walks the cons-chain until outer position $k$ sits at the head, then inspects that cons-cell's `WalkStep`. The slot-of-interest fires only on the `.backwardE _` constructor ‚Äî which encodes $(v_k, v_{k-1}) \in G.E$ with $v_k$ as tail, i.e. the LN's $v_k \tuh v_{k-1}$ outgoing arrow. `.forwardE _` (incoming E-edge) and `.bidir _` (L-edge) return `False`, matching the canonical-tex E-only reading of $\tuh$ and the `outOfStart` / `outOfEnd` precedent in `Walks.lean`. The `1 ‚â§ k` guard from the Option-membership original collapses into the `(.cons _ _ _, 0) ‚Üí False` branch (no slot at $k = 0$); out-of-range $k$ bottom out at `.nil _ _`. Index arithmetic: outer slot $i = k - 1$ on the cons-tail becomes tail slot $i = (k - 1) - 1$, hence the recursive call passes `k + 1` at outer `k + 2`.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- Net-new helper under the typed-WalkStep refactor: decides whether the slot `i = k - 1` step is an outgoing E-walk-edge of v_k whose other endpoint exits `G.Sc v_k`.
def HasBlockingLeftSlot : ‚àÄ {u v : Node}, Walk G u v ‚Üí ‚Ñï ‚Üí Prop
  -- Trivial walk: no edges, no slot to inspect ‚Äî always `False`.
  | _, _, .nil _ _, _ => False
  -- Outer position `k = 0`: slot `i = -1` does not exist, structural encoding of the original's `1 ‚â§ k` guard.
  | _, _, .cons _ _ _, 0 => False
  -- Slot-of-interest at outer `k = 1`: `.backwardE _` encodes `(v_k, v_{k-1}) ‚àà G.E` (v_k as tail); fires iff `u = v_{k-1}` exits `G.Sc v`.
  | u, _, .cons v (.backwardE _) _, 1 => u ‚àâ G.Sc v
  -- `.forwardE` is an incoming E-edge of v_k (wrong direction for the LN's `\tuh`) ‚Äî never blocks at this slot.
  | _, _, .cons _ (.forwardE _) _, 1 => False
  -- `.bidir` is an L-edge (wrong channel ‚Äî LN's `\tuh` is E-only) ‚Äî never blocks at this slot.
  | _, _, .cons _ (.bidir _) _, 1 => False
  -- Outer slot `i = k - 1` on the cons-tail walk becomes tail slot `i = (k - 1) - 1`; recursive descent decrements the index by 1.
  | _, _, .cons _ _ p, k + 2 => p.HasBlockingLeftSlot (k + 1)

lean_blocks.code:
def HasBlockingRightSlot : ‚àÄ {u v : Node}, Walk G u v ‚Üí ‚Ñï ‚Üí Prop
  | _, _, .nil _ _, _ => False
  | u, _, .cons v (.forwardE _) _, 0 => v ‚àâ G.Sc u
  | _, _, .cons _ (.backwardE _) _, 0 => False
  | _, _, .cons _ (.bidir _) _, 0 => False
  | _, _, .cons _ _ p, k + 1 => p.HasBlockingRightSlot k
lean_blocks.explanation: Mirror of `HasBlockingLeftSlot` on the slot $i = k$ side: decides whether the WalkStep that STARTS at outer position $k$ is an outgoing E-walk-edge of $v_k$ whose other walk-endpoint lies outside $G.Sc\, v_k$. At outer position $k = 0$ the slot of interest is the head cons-cell's step directly ‚Äî no recursion needed ‚Äî so the three constructor branches sit at index `0`. The slot fires only on `.forwardE _`, encoding $(v_k, v_{k+1}) \in G.E$ with $v_k$ as tail (the LN's $v_k \tuh v_{k+1}$); `.backwardE _` would be incoming and `.bidir _` would be L-channel, both rejected. Self-loops $(v, v) \in G.E$ bind both indices of the cons-cell to $v$, so the check $v \notin G.Sc\, v$ evaluates `False` by `def_3_5`'s self-membership, absorbing the canonical tex's self-loop semantics without special-casing. Index arithmetic: outer slot $i = k$ on the tail becomes tail slot $i = k - 1$, hence the recursive call passes `k` at outer `k + 1`.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- Net-new helper: decides whether the slot `i = k` step (the one that STARTS at outer position `k`) is an outgoing E-walk-edge of v_k exiting `G.Sc v_k`.
def HasBlockingRightSlot : ‚àÄ {u v : Node}, Walk G u v ‚Üí ‚Ñï ‚Üí Prop
  -- Trivial walk: no edges, no slot to inspect ‚Äî always `False`.
  | _, _, .nil _ _, _ => False
  -- Slot-of-interest at outer `k = 0`: `.forwardE _` encodes `(v_k, v_{k+1}) ‚àà G.E` (v_k as tail); fires iff `v = v_{k+1}` exits `G.Sc u`.
  | u, _, .cons v (.forwardE _) _, 0 => v ‚àâ G.Sc u
  -- `.backwardE` is an incoming E-edge of v_k (wrong direction for the LN's `\tuh`) ‚Äî never blocks at this slot.
  | _, _, .cons _ (.backwardE _) _, 0 => False
  -- `.bidir` is an L-edge (wrong channel ‚Äî LN's `\tuh` is E-only) ‚Äî never blocks at this slot.
  | _, _, .cons _ (.bidir _) _, 0 => False
  -- Outer slot `i = k` on the cons-tail walk becomes tail slot `i = k - 1`; recursive descent decrements the index by 1.
  | _, _, .cons _ _ p, k + 1 => p.HasBlockingRightSlot k

lean_blocks.code:
def IsBlockableNonCollider {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) (k : ‚Ñï) : Prop :=
  p.IsNonCollider k ‚àß
  ( k = 0 ‚à® k = p.length ‚à®
    p.HasBlockingLeftSlot k ‚à®
    p.HasBlockingRightSlot k )
lean_blocks.explanation: The primary positive predicate: position $k$ on `p` is a *blockable non-collider* iff `p.IsNonCollider k` (the LN's non-collider precondition) AND the four-disjunct blockable disjunction holds. The four disjuncts mirror the canonical-tex spelled-out form word-for-word: end-position `k = 0`, end-position `k = p.length`, slot-$(k-1)$ blocking witness (delegated to `HasBlockingLeftSlot`), slot-$k$ blocking witness (delegated to `HasBlockingRightSlot`). All recursion lives inside the two helpers, so this declaration stays flat and reads off the LN directly. The `IsNonCollider` conjunct is load-bearing: without it, a collider admitting a `.backwardE` or `.forwardE` step exiting $G.Sc\, v_k$ would be mis-classified as blockable, but the LN restricts the classification to the non-collider sub-class. End-positions automatically satisfy both the non-collider precondition and one of the first two disjuncts.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- Primary positive predicate: non-collider precondition AND the LN's four-disjunct blockable disjunction (two end-position + two slot-of-interest).
def IsBlockableNonCollider {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) (k : ‚Ñï) : Prop :=
  -- Non-collider precondition: load-bearing, encodes the LN's restriction of the classification to the non-collider sub-class.
  p.IsNonCollider k ‚àß
  -- End-position disjuncts (k = 0 or k = p.length) followed by the slot-(k-1) and slot-k helpers; recursion lives inside the helpers.
  ( k = 0 ‚à® k = p.length ‚à®
    p.HasBlockingLeftSlot k ‚à®
    p.HasBlockingRightSlot k )

lean_blocks.code:
def IsUnblockableNonCollider {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) (k : ‚Ñï) : Prop :=
  p.IsNonCollider k ‚àß ¬¨ p.IsBlockableNonCollider k
lean_blocks.explanation: The derived predicate: a position `k` on `p` is an *unblockable non-collider* iff it is a non-collider AND not a blockable non-collider. Unfolding the negation of `IsBlockableNonCollider`'s disjunction recovers the LN's two-implication unblockable characterisation: `k` is interior (`k ‚âÝ 0 ‚àß k ‚âÝ p.length`, the negation of the end-position disjuncts), and for each walk-incident slot, if the walk's edge there is a directed edge with tail `v_k`, then its other walk-endpoint lies in `G.Sc vk`. Mutual exclusivity on the non-collider sub-class is therefore definitional rather than an external theorem: for any `k` with `p.IsNonCollider k`, exactly one of `IsBlockableNonCollider k` and `IsUnblockableNonCollider k` holds by unfolding. The `p.IsNonCollider k` conjunct is load-bearing ‚Äî without it, every collider would automatically satisfy `¬¨ p.IsBlockableNonCollider k` (since blockable carries the non-collider precondition as its first conjunct), mis-classifying every collider as unblockable. Dot-notation `p.IsBlockableNonCollider k` resolves to `Walk.IsBlockableNonCollider p k` from the same namespace declared one block above.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- The derived predicate: non-collider precondition AND the negation of `IsBlockableNonCollider` on the same position.
def IsUnblockableNonCollider {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) (k : ‚Ñï) : Prop :=
  -- Non-collider conjunct is load-bearing ‚Äî without it, colliders would vacuously satisfy `¬¨ IsBlockableNonCollider`.
  p.IsNonCollider k ‚àß ¬¨ p.IsBlockableNonCollider k

design_choices:
**Blockable is the primary positive predicate; unblockable is its negation on the non-collider sub-class.** The LN's blockable elaboration is itself a disjunction ‚Äî end-position or some outgoing walk-edge of $v_k$ to a node outside $\mathrm{Sc}^G(v_k)$ ‚Äî so `IsBlockableNonCollider` reads off the LN as a flat four-disjunct, and `IsUnblockableNonCollider := p.IsNonCollider k ‚àß ¬¨ p.IsBlockableNonCollider k` makes mutual exclusivity definitional. The positive witness is what downstream walk-reversal proofs (claim_3_22 onward) manipulate; the universal-implication form for unblockable would duplicate the case-split with negated polarities and owe an external proof.

**Slot-`(k-1)` and slot-`k` queries factor into the recursive helpers `HasBlockingLeftSlot` / `HasBlockingRightSlot`.** Under the typed-WalkStep refactor (`def_3_4`) `Walk.edges` is intentionally absent, so the original's Option-valued `p.edges[k - 1]?` / `p.edges[k]?` lookups have no counterpart; channel/direction are now carried by `WalkStep`'s constructor tag (`.forwardE` / `.backwardE` / `.bidir`) plus its type indices. Per-slot inspection therefore descends the cons-chain and pattern-matches on the WalkStep, mirroring `IsCollider` / `outOfStart` / `outOfEnd`. The factoring keeps `IsBlockableNonCollider` a flat four-disjunct (`k = 0 ‚à® k = p.length ‚à® HasBlockingLeftSlot k ‚à® HasBlockingRightSlot k`) mirroring the canonical-tex disjunction.

**Constructor-tag-only at the slot of interest; `.bidir` rejected, no writing-mirror union.** `HasBlockingLeftSlot` fires only on `.backwardE _` at slot $i = k - 1$ (encoding $(v_k, v_{k-1}) \in G.E$ with $v_k$ as tail); `HasBlockingRightSlot` fires only on `.forwardE _` at slot $i = k$. The LN's $\tuh$ shorthand (def_3_2 item 2) is strictly $E$-membership, so $L$-edges contribute no outgoing arrowheads ‚Äî unlike `IsInto`, whose underlying primitive is a union over $E$ and $L$ and needs a writing-mirror disjunct.

**Self-loops absorbed by `Sc` self-membership, not special-cased.** A directed self-loop $(v, v) \in G.E$ at the slot of interest binds both cons-cell indices to $v$; the check $v \notin G.Sc\, v$ evaluates to `False` because every vertex is trivially in its own SC component (def_3_5's trivial-walk witness). Matches the canonical tex's "Treatment of directed self-loops" byte-for-byte.

**Range bounds collapse into the structural recursion.** The original's explicit `1 ‚â§ k` guard becomes `HasBlockingLeftSlot`'s `(.cons _ _ _, 0) ‚Üí False` branch; out-of-range $k > p.\mathrm{length}$ bottoms out at `.nil _ _`. End-positions are placed in the blockable class via the first two disjuncts of `IsBlockableNonCollider`, matching the LN's $k \in \{0, n\}$ disjunct.

**`p.IsNonCollider k` conjunct is load-bearing on both definitions.** Without it, `IsBlockableNonCollider` over-fires on colliders admitting a blocking WalkStep, and `IsUnblockableNonCollider` over-fires on every collider (which automatically satisfies the negation since blockable carries the non-collider precondition as its first conjunct). `G.Sc` is reused from `def_3_5`.
